steps.title: Identify what the underlined portion does
steps.content: The underlined phrase — "a phenomenon that occurs when the same trait evolves independently in two reproductively separate lineages" — is a parenthetical inserted immediately after "convergent evolution." It defines the term in plain language before the text goes on to describe how Protas, Moczek, Green, and Extavour each studied it.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It clarifies a concept that the author implies was unclear in the studies mentioned in the text.': The passage never implies the studies were unclear; the definition is given for the reader’s benefit, not to correct a flaw in the research. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It provides examples of how a phenomenon was studied by scientists in the field before Green and Extavour’s study.': A one-sentence definition is not an example of how scientists studied something; the Protas and Moczek studies serve that role later. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It introduces a method of scientific analysis that is discussed in greater detail later in the text.': Convergent evolution is a phenomenon, not a method of analysis, and the parenthetical defines it rather than introducing an analytical technique. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It gives a basic description of a phenomenon that is central to the discussion that follows.': The phrase is a concise definition of convergent evolution, the concept that every study in the passage examines. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
